topic: bullied orange serum revenge cafeteria drama

template.label: Anthropomorphic Fruit Revenge Drama
template.description: Continuity-sensitive five-beat fruit revenge short built from locked cast sheets, scene start frames, visible proof, absurd justice, and a cliffhanger-ready ending.

authoring_sections.title: Format Summary
authoring_sections.instruction: Describe the fruit revenge concept, the triggering injustice, the absurd justice mechanism, and the cliffhanger payoff in 2-4 sentences. State why the cast contrast and revenge turn are legible at a glance on Shorts/Reels/TikTok.

authoring_sections.title: Fruit Cast Sheet
authoring_sections.instruction: List every recurring fruit character by fruit type, name, role, outfit, emotional energy, and locked visual details. Make the wronged lead, target, and any ally or witness instantly distinguishable.

authoring_sections.title: Justice Mechanic
authoring_sections.instruction: Explain the concrete revenge device, prop, or public reveal that makes the payoff absurd but satisfying. Keep it visual, simple, and safe for short-form viewing.

authoring_sections.title: Location Lock
authoring_sections.instruction: Name the recurring locations and specify the props, lighting logic, and color accents that should stay consistent across clips.

authoring_sections.title: Asset Plan
authoring_sections.instruction: Specify which portrait assets, turnaround/reference sheets, and scene start frames must exist before rendering. Mention which scenes need multiple character references attached and which clip lands the cliffhanger.

required_steps: define the recurring fruit cast and the injustice that triggers the revenge
generate one portrait and one turnaround/reference sheet per recurring character
author a five-beat revenge markdown from saved repo state
generate one scene start frame per clip using cast references
animate each scene frame into a short clip with simple dialogue
stitch clips, burn captions, and save the final run artifacts
workflow_contract.scene_generation_mode: one scene card per clip with image-first continuity locking

rules: Match the exact same fruit identity and face geometry as the approved hero portrait.
Do not redesign the character into a different fruit type, outfit, or silhouette.
Keep the same proportions, palette, material finish, and wardrobe while changing only the presentation into turnaround-sheet form.

preamble: Treat the following image direction as the opening frame and visual identity to preserve.
Continuity is more important than novelty or extra detail.

If recurring characters are present, keep the exact same face/head design, silhouette, wardrobe logic, proportions, palette, environment, and cinematic style established by the image direction and continuity anchors.
Do not redesign characters into generic humans, new costumes, or a different art style.
If dialogue makes the acting feel weak, keep the acting simple and let the visual beat stay dominant.
lines.default_continuity_anchors: Preserve the same identity, wardrobe, set, and overall style established by the image prompt.
lines.motion_intro: Animate that exact scene using this motion and dialogue direction:
lines.speaker_named: Only {{speaker}} may deliver the spoken line in this clip.
lines.speaker_none: If a spoken line is present, keep it assigned to one clearly readable speaker.
lines.speaker_lock_named: Speaker lock: {{speaker}} is the only character allowed to produce audible speech, lip-sync, whisper words, narrate, or mouth the dialogue.
lines.speaker_lock_none: If no named speaker is provided, prefer no audible dialogue over assigning the line to the wrong character.
lines.silent_characters_named: {{silent_characters}} stay on-screen silent and must not mouth, lip-sync, whisper, narrate, or appear to say the line.
lines.silent_characters_none: Any other visible characters stay silent unless the prompt explicitly says otherwise.
lines.dialogue_none: Use no spoken dialogue unless the motion direction explicitly requires it.
lines.wrong_speaker_failsafe_named: If the line cannot be cleanly assigned to {{speaker}}, output no audible dialogue instead of giving the line to another character.
lines.wrong_speaker_failsafe_none: Do not let the wrong character deliver the line.
lines.staging_named: Keep staging and face visibility clear so {{speaker}} is the obvious readable source of the line when speech occurs.
lines.staging_none: Keep staging clear so any speaking source is visually unambiguous.
ending: Keep the framing, character, body-part context, and action clear and legible.
Output a single finished video clip.

role_blueprint.energy: sympathetic, bruised but observant, then increasingly sharp and in control
smug, flashy, dismissive, ridiculous once exposed
grounded, useful, helps reveal the truth or trigger the payoff

scene_contract.scene_prompt_strategy: each clip should open from a continuity-locked scene frame, then animate one dominant revenge beat with clear staging, one named speaker at most, explicit silent-character control, a visible justice turn, and a final sting that invites the next episode

distilled_template_guidance.format_hint: anthropomorphic fruit revenge story told as five tight cinematic beats with a locked fruit cast, visible injustice, satisfying payback, and a cliffhanger sting
distilled_template_guidance.voice_direction: absurd but emotionally clear, one named speaker per clip, explicit silent-character control, mobile-readable justice beats, one dominant action per scene, cliffhanger-ready ending
research_notes: Lock the recurring fruit cast with one approved hero portrait and one derived reference sheet per main character before scene generation.
Keep fruit identity, facial geometry, wardrobe, body proportions, and color coding fixed across all clips.
Make the original wrongdoing and the revenge payoff visually legible through props, staging, and one clear turn per scene.
Use scene start frames with attached cast references whenever multiple fruit characters appear together.
Favor short dialogue and let the trap, reveal, or public humiliation carry the satisfaction.
Let the final beat land justice first, then add a cliffhanger sting that implies the world is not fully done escalating.
creative_direction: Use polished stylized 3D fruit characters with premium rind texture, expressive cartoon-human eyes, strong silhouette contrast, and cinematic mobile-readable staging.
Make the wronged lead sympathetic at a glance and the target visibly smug, slippery, or ridiculous before the tables turn.

Keep revenge mechanisms absurd but concrete: giant dessert trap, humiliating juice spill, booby-trapped stage reveal, collapsing tower of lies, or a public fruit-market payoff that reads instantly on mobile.
Preserve one recurring hero location and one payoff location so the world feels stable while the justice escalates.
video_examples: Clip 1 should show the humiliating wrong in one clear image beat.
Clip 3 should make the revenge setup visible before the justice lands.
Clip 5 should feel satisfying first, then end with a stinger that implies a bigger aftermath.
